function.name: High-Level: PropsSI
function.description: Calculates the state dependent property specified by "Output" given the input property pair "Input1" and "Input2", with their respective values passed in Value1 and Value2, for the specified "Fluid". Output and Input name strings represent a thermodynamic property (i.e., "T", "P", "D", "H", "C", "V", "L", "Q", etc.). Only one property can be specified for the Output string and each of the Input strings. For a complete list of valid property names, see the CoolProp documentation at https:\\coolprop.org.

function.name: High-Level: PropsSImulti
function.description: Calculates the state dependent properties specified by "Outputs" given the input property pair "Input1" and "Input2", with their respective values passed in arrays Vec1 and Vec2, for the specified "Fluid". The the two numerical inputs, Vec1 and Vec2, MUST be column (vertical) vectors of the same length. "Outputs" and "Input Name" contain strings representing a thermodynamic property (i.e., "T", "P", "D", "H", "C", "V", "L", "Q", etc.) Multiple "Outputs" can be returned by specifying the requested properties in a space, comma, or semicolon delimited string (e.g. "D Hmass Smass"). Results are returned in a vector/matrix with state point pairs on each row and specified output values across each column. The return matrix should be checked and or filtered for NaN values, indicating an error in calculating that particular state point value. For a complete list of valid property names for Inputs and Outputs, see the CoolProp documentation at https:\\coolprop.org.

function.name: High-Level: PhaseSI
function.description: Determines the state dependent phase (returned as a "string") given the input property pair "Input1" and "Input2", with their respective values passed in Value1 and Value2, for the specified "Fluid". Input names are strings representing a thermodynamic property (i.e., "T", "P", "D", "H", "C", "V", "L", "Q", etc.) For a complete list of valid property names, see the CoolProp documentation at https:\\coolprop.org.

function.name: High-Level: Props1SI
function.description: Returns the non-state dependent fluid property specified by "Property" for the specified "Fluid". Property names are strings representing a trivial fluid property (i.e., "Tcrit", "Ptriple", "GAS_CONSTANT", etc.). For a complete list of valid property names, see the CoolProp documentation at https:\\coolprop.org.

function.name: Humid Air: HAPropsSI
function.description: Calculates the state dependent property specified by "Output" for Humid Air given the input properties "Input1", "Input2", and "Input3", with their respective scalar values passed in Value1, Value2, and Value3. Output and Input names are strings representing a thermodynamic property (i.e., "T", "P", "D", "H", "C", "V", "L", "Q", "R", etc.). One of these Input names and values is typically "R" specifying a relative humidity. For a complete list of valid property names, see the CoolProp documentation at https:\\coolprop.org.

function.name: Low-Level: Get Global Parameter
function.description: Returns the value of the requested CoolProps parameter. Useful parameter strings are "FluidList", to return a comma separated list of all valid fluid name strings, and "version", which returns the installed version of CoolProp.

function.name: Low-Level: Get Fluid Parameter
function.description: Returns the value of the requested fluid parameter. Useful parameter strings are "aliases", to return a comma separated list of valid fluid name strings, and "CAS", which returns the fluids Chemical Abstract Service registration number.

function.name: Low-Level: Set Reference State
function.description: Sets the reference state for a specific fluid one of the standard reference states: "IIR", "ASHRAE", "NBP", or "DEF" (CoolProp default).

function.name: Low-Level: Get Mixture Binary Pair
function.description: Gets the binary mixing parameter for a specified binary pair. Fluid names MUST be the "CAS" string for each of the components. Parameter must be one of ["name1", "name2", "function", "type", "F", "xi", "zeta", "gammaT", "gammaV", "betaT", "betaV"].

function.name: Low-Level: Set Mixture Binary Pair
function.description: Sets the binary mixing parameter for a specified binary pair. Fluid names MUST be the "CAS" string for each of the components. "Parameter" must be one of ["F", "xi", "zeta", "gammaT", "gammaV", "betaT", "betaV"]. Value, is the numeric value of the parameter being set.

function.name: Low-Level: Get PCSAFT Mixture Binary Pair
function.description: Gets the PCSAFT binary mixing parameter for a specified binary pair. Fluid names MUST be the "CAS" string for each of the components. The parameter being retrieved can be either the constant parameter "kij" or the temperature coefficient "kijT".

function.name: Low-Level: Set PCSAFT Mixture Binary Pair
function.description: Sets the PCSAFT binary mixing parameter for a specified binary pair. Fluid names are identified by the "CAS" string for each of the components. The parameter being set can be either the constant parameter "kij" or the temperature coefficient "kijT". Value, is the numeric value of the parameter being set.

function.name: Low-Level: Apply Simple Mixing Rule
function.description: Sets the binary mixing rule for a specified binary pair. Fluid names can be either the "CAS" string or one of the fluid alias strings for each of the components. "Rule" must be either "linear" (to set a linear function of molar composition) or "Lorentz-Berthelot" (all interaction parameters set to 1.0).
